title: Question: Global disable of /*verilator public*/
body:
---


Author Name: **Jonathon Donaldson**
Original Redmine Message: 1628 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Is there a way to globally disable/override all /*verilator public*/ signals (e.g. with a command line switch)? A good use for this is if I want to have a high-performance build of the design which doesn't require any verilator public access and another build where I actually perform debug/verification of internal signals.

The only way I can think of to do this write now is to define a verilog macro and use it in the code as follows:

```
wire my_signal
`ifndef HIGH_PERF_MODE
/*verilator public*/
`endif ;

```

But that makes the code look really ugly if you have a lot of signals that you need access to. Seems like there should be a way to do it on the command line that just tells verilator to ignore all /*verilator public*/ directives. That way you could simply have one build script for high performance mode and another for debugging mode. Is this what --private is supposed to do? It sounded from the manual that --public and --private are legacy so wasn't sure if they should be used.
